prompt: I'm stuck between a single orchestrator, a state-machine-driven workflow, and a set of thin skills coordinated by handoffs for a new repository-maintenance feature. I haven't decided whether resumability or simplicity matters more yet. Help me figure out the architecture options before we commit to implementation.
assumptions: No repository or implementation code was provided, so the response stays at architecture level.
The feature includes both read-only analysis and some mutating repository actions.
Exact resumability may matter later, but is not yet confirmed as a hard requirement.
Auditability and safe retries matter, but likely below full compliance-workflow levels for v1.
